shadows.name: launch-hover-glow
shadows.purpose: Hover state on the primary action button only. Signals 'press this.' Never reused on other elements.

shadows.name: active-inset-marker
shadows.purpose: Left-edge active state indicator on the selected game list item. Uses inset so item width never shifts.

shadows.name: modal-scrim
shadows.purpose: Backdrop overlay behind the Add Custom Game modal. Background color on the modal-backdrop element.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default transition duration and easing for all interactive state changes (color, background, border, opacity).

motion.name: fast-transition
motion.purpose: Faster transitions for high-frequency elements like game list item hover states.

motion.name: spin
motion.purpose: Loading spinner animation. Only during initial game list fetch.

motion.name: dot-scan
motion.purpose: Opacity pulse on the scan-amber status dot. Gate with prefers-reduced-motion. The only ambient animation in the UI.

components.name: Launch Button (Rest)
components.description: Primary action button. Launches the selected game process. Full-width within launch area.
components.css: .ds-btn-launch { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; flex: 1; height: 44px; background: #00c2ff; color: #000000; border: none; border-radius: 8px; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s, box-shadow 0.15s; } .ds-btn-launch:hover { background: #00a8dd; box-shadow: 0 2px 12px rgba(0,194,255,0.15); } .ds-btn-launch:disabled { opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Launch Button (Active / Running)
components.description: Launch button state when a game process is running. No animation — the color change is the entire signal.
components.css: .ds-btn-launch { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; flex: 1; height: 44px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s; } .ds-btn-launch--active { background: #00e676; color: #000000; } .ds-btn-launch--active:hover { background: #00c960; }

components.name: Stop Button
components.description: Destructive ghost button. Appears alongside the active launch button to end a game session.
components.css: .ds-btn-stop { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 44px; padding: 0 22px; background: #1e2024; border: 1px solid #2a2d33; color: #f23f42; border-radius: 8px; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-btn-stop:hover { background: #2a2d33; }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Sidebar game search field. Full-width, 13px body font, focus shifts border to Ice Channel.
components.css: .ds-input-search { width: 100%; padding: 7px 10px; background: #1e2024; border: 1px solid #2a2d33; border-radius: 8px; color: #e8e9ea; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; font-size: 13px; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.15s; } .ds-input-search:focus { border-color: #00c2ff; } .ds-input-search::placeholder { color: #4a4f57; }

components.name: Game List Item
components.description: 44px list item in the sidebar game list. Virtual scrolled at scale. Color avatar + truncated game name.
components.css: .ds-game-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 9px; height: 44px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: 6px; margin: 1px 6px; cursor: pointer; position: relative; transition: background 0.1s; } .ds-game-item:hover { background: #1e2024; } .ds-game-item:hover .ds-game-name { color: #ffffff; } .ds-game-avatar { width: 28px; height: 28px; border-radius: 6px; flex-shrink: 0; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.75); } .ds-game-name { font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; color: #c8cdd4; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; transition: color 0.1s; }

components.name: Game List Item (Active)
components.description: Active/selected state of a game list item. Ice Channel tint background with inset left-edge marker.
components.html:
C
Clash Royale
components.css: .ds-game-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 9px; height: 44px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: 6px; margin: 1px 6px; cursor: pointer; position: relative; } .ds-game-item--active { background: rgba(0,194,255,0.09); box-shadow: inset 2px 0 0 #00c2ff; } .ds-game-item--active .ds-game-name { color: #ffffff; } .ds-game-avatar { width: 28px; height: 28px; border-radius: 6px; flex-shrink: 0; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.75); } .ds-game-name { font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; } .ds-game-dot { position: absolute; right: 10px; top: 50%; transform: translateY(-50%); width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; background: #00e676; }

components.name: Stat Box
components.description: 2-up grid in the game detail view. Shows Status and Elapsed Time. No shadows.
components.html:
Status
Live
components.css: .ds-stat-box { background: #1e2024; border: 1px solid #1e2024; border-radius: 8px; padding: 13px 16px; } .ds-stat-label { font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #4a4f57; margin-bottom: 7px; } .ds-stat-value { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; color: #ffffff; } .ds-status-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-status-dot--live { background: #00e676; } .ds-status-dot--scanning { background: #f0b429; animation: ds-dot-scan 0.7s ease-in-out infinite; } .ds-status-dot--idle { background: #4a4f57; } .ds-stat-text--live { color: #00e676; } .ds-stat-text--scanning { color: #f0b429; } .ds-stat-text--idle { color: #4a4f57; } @keyframes ds-dot-scan { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; } 50% { opacity: 0.35; } }

components.name: Exe Badge
components.description: Monospace chip below the game title showing the .exe filename Discord expects. Data tag, not a UI label.
components.css: .ds-exe-badge { display: inline-block; padding: 3px 8px; background: #0d0d0f; border: 1px solid #1e2024; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'Consolas', 'Courier New', monospace; font-size: 11px; color: #4a9eff; line-height: 1.4; }

narrative.overview:
PresencePal is a status instrument, not a dashboard. Its job is to read state at a glance and execute actions in under ten seconds. Every design decision derives from that constraint: what earns screen space must communicate something, and what does not communicate is removed. The aesthetic is calibrated, not styled — like a rack-mounted hardware monitor or a well-configured terminal, the surface is functional by nature.

The palette is near-monochrome by design. Three dark backgrounds form a quiet depth stack; color appears only where state demands it. Ice Channel cyan marks the live path — focused input, active selection, the brand word that means 'this works.' Green means running. Amber means scanning. Red means stop. These four colors are information, not decoration, and they carry that responsibility exclusively.

The app lives beside Discord all day. It is designed to look like it belongs to a different, quieter category — not a Discord plugin, not a gaming tool, not a SaaS interface. The frame is invisible; the status is everything.
keyCharacteristics: Near-monochrome dark field with four semantic state colors
Status-first: running indicators, elapsed timers, and scanning feedback are the primary visual hierarchy
44px as the universal touch target across game items, launch, and stop
No decorative chrome; every element is functional or absent
System-UI typography at neutral weight — readable, zero branding overhead

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Ice Channel is the only accent. It carries the active path, the brand word, and focused state. Any other use dilutes the signal. When in doubt, use Muted Text, not the accent.

rules.name: The Four-Color Doctrine
rules.body: Status is expressed in exactly four colors: Cyan (active path), Green (running), Amber (scanning), Red (stop). No other color encodes state. A new state must map to one of these four or the doctrine must be explicitly revised.

rules.name: The Mono Boundary Rule
rules.body: Monospace appears only for technical data the user might copy: exe filenames and elapsed time digits. It does not appear in nav labels, headings, or button text.

rules.name: The Tonal Lift Rule
rules.body: New surfaces are expressed by stepping one level up the bg ramp (depth-black → studio-dark → elevated-surface). Shadows are prohibited as a depth mechanism. The only allowed shadow is the launch hover glow, which is state feedback, not elevation.

dos: Do use Ice Channel only where the user is told 'this is the active path' — focused inputs, selected game items, the Launch button, the 'Pal' wordmark.
Do express elevation through the three-step tonal ramp (depth-black → studio-dark → elevated-surface). No shadows on surfaces at rest.
Do keep all interactive targets at 44px height — game items, Launch, Stop, modal action buttons.
Do use the four semantic state colors (Cyan, Green, Amber, Red) to communicate exactly one concept each, never for decoration.
Do use box-shadow: inset 2px 0 0 #00c2ff for left-edge state markers so item width never shifts.
Do use Consolas exclusively for exe filenames and elapsed time digits.
Do keep transitions at 0.1-0.15s with ease-out easing. State changes are immediate feedback, not choreography.
Do gate the scan dot opacity animation behind prefers-reduced-motion: reduce. It is the only ambient loop in the UI.
donts: Don't add blurple, pill buttons, or server-sidebar layouts. PresencePal works beside Discord, not inside it.
Don't use neon gradients, glow grids, or Razer-style visual noise. The user's gaming setup is already loud — this app is the calm instrument they reach for.
Don't apply hero metrics, big-number stat cards with gradient accents, or Figma-template card grids. No generic SaaS dashboard patterns.
Don't use border-left greater than 1px as a colored stripe accent. Use inset box-shadow or tinted background instead.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with a gradient). Emphasis through weight or size only.
Don't use glassmorphism: no blurs, no frosted surfaces, no backdrop-filter.
Don't add shadows to surfaces at rest. The launch hover glow is the only allowed shadow; it exists to signal 'this is the primary action.'
Don't animate layout properties (height, width, top, left, padding). Transitions apply to color, background, opacity, and box-shadow only.
Don't introduce a fifth state color. The Four-Color Doctrine is structural; map new states to the existing four or revise the doctrine explicitly.
Don't use Ice Channel for decorative fills, background washes, or hover highlights on non-interactive elements. Its rarity is its meaning.
